questions.question: A shirt originally costs $40. It is on sale for 25% off. What is the sale price?
A restaurant bill is $50. You leave a 20% tip. How much tip do you leave?
Last year, a company had 200 employees. This year, it has 240 employees. What is the percent increase in employees?
A book costs $60 after a 40% discount. What was the original price?
questions.explanation: If 40% is discounted, you pay 60% of the original price. So 0.60 × original = 60, giving original = $100.

questions.question: A store increases the price of a toy from $20 to $25. What is the percent increase?
A jacket is marked 30% off. If the original price is $80, what do you pay?
